nodes.name: enable_incremental_state_configuration
nodes.description: Produces an incremental-state-enabled class/configuration as a pure class-level transformation.

outputs.name: incremental_state_cls
outputs.constraints: Configured class/object representing incremental-state behavior without hidden mutation.

nodes.conceptual_summary: deterministic_type_lifting_for_incremental_transition_semantics. Maps a base type descriptor to a derived type descriptor that supports explicit incremental state transitions. The mapping is applied at class-definition level, so behavior is extended structurally rather than by hidden runtime mutation. The original type remains unchanged, and the transform behaves as a referentially transparent type-to-type constructor.. Applications: Robotics: convert control-module definitions into stepwise update variants that consume sensor deltas and emit explicit next-state artifacts., Numerical simulation: lift solver classes into timestep-incremental forms for reproducible checkpoint-to-checkpoint field evolution., Signal processing: transform batch filter definitions into streaming operators with explicit carried state across data chunks., Distributed systems: adapt protocol handlers into event-incremental processors that externalize state progression for replay and auditing.
